# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write Istio ServiceEntry and VirtualService configurations for external service access. Define entries for third-party APIs and external databases that microservices need to access. Configure DNS resolution and traffic routing to external hosts. This is for our service mesh egress configuration.
# Category: istio_egress_unrestricted

apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1alpha3
kind: DestinationPolicy
metadata:
 name: outbound|myexternalservice_defaultURI
 namespace: default-namespace  # This should match the actual namespaces in your mesh if you're using multiple Namespace configurations across a set of pods or microservices; for multitenancy, use "istio-injection" key. (Optional) Default is current Istio namespace unless otherwise specified
 spec: {}  
---  # This represents the rule that sets destination rules in your mesh/namespace to route requests outside control plane components such as service entries and virtual services through a single sided sidecar or outbound http(s). For more details, refer https://istio.io/latest/docs/setup/additional-configuration#loadbalancing
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1alpha3  # This matches DestinationPolicy kind in the above definition (Optional) Default is current Istio namespace unless otherwise specified; for multitenancy, use "namespace" key which also applies to all configurations within that scope of namespaces are used
kind: SidecarSelectorRule   rule selection strategy and filtering rules set on mesh side cars. This enables more complex routing scenarios at the service level where you have control over when a request reaches its destination using selectors in conjunction with out-of-box load balancing strategies such as least connections or leiasing, etc (Optional) Defaults to default rule unless otherwise specified
metadata:  name: "default"   # Name of this policy. If not given and the mesh has multiple policies configured it must be set here so you can select which one is active with `istioctl` commands like `--selector=<name>` (Optional) Defaults to default rule unless otherwise specified
spec:  selector: {}    This should match a service or group of services in your control plane that the sidecar will route traffic for. For example, "app1" can select all pods running under namespace 'ns-A' with labels app=myApp and version below v2 but not above (Optional) Default to empty selector which means no specific selection
